[Footers](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/footers)\\n- [Header navigations](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/header-navigations)\\n- [Hero header posts](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/blog-post-pages)\\n- [Blogs](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/blog-pages)\\n- [Contact pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/contact-pages)\\n- [FAQ pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/faq-pages)\\n- [Landing pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/landing-pages)\\n- [Legal pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/legal-pages)\\n- [Pricing pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/pricing-pages)\\n- [Team pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/team-pages)\\n- [404 pages](https://www.untitledui.com/react/marketing/404-pages)\\n\\n1. [Base components](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components)\\n2. [Components](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components)\\n3. Buttons\\n\\n[X](https://x.com/UntitledUI)[1,696](https://github.com/untitleduico/react)\\n\\n[Sign in](https://www.untitledui.com/login) [Get PRO](https://www.untitledui.com/buy/react)\\n\\n# Button components\\n\\nFREE\\n\\nCopy [Button groups](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/button-groups) [Checkboxes](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/checkboxes)\\n\\nFree and open-source React button components built for modern applications and websites. These buttons are built using React Aria and styled with Tailwind CSS.\\n\\n- [GitHub](https://github.com/untitleduico/react/tree/main/components/base/buttons)\\n- [React Aria](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Button.html)\\n\\n### [Button example](about:blank\\\\#button-example)\\n\\n### [Button example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#button-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nDelete projectStage for publishPublish now\\n\\n## [Installation](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#installation)\\n\\nYou can add this button component using our CLI or manually:\\n\\nCLI\\n\\nManual\\n\\n```\\nnpx untitledui@latest add button\\n```\\n\\n## [Button examples](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#button-examples)\\n\\nBelow are examples and variations of this button component:\\n\\n### [Primary buttons](about:blank\\\\#primary-buttons)\\n\\n### [Primary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#primary-buttons)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Secondary buttons](about:blank\\\\#secondary-buttons)\\n\\n### [Secondary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#secondary-buttons)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Tertiary buttons](about:blank\\\\#tertiary-buttons)\\n\\n### [Tertiary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#tertiary-buttons)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Link color buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#link-color-buttons)\\n\\nWe've designed our button components to be \\\"hybrid,\\\" meaning they can function as either a link or a standard button with ease. This offers several benefits, for example, there are many situations where you might want a link that behaves like a button, or a button that acts as a link. These two essential components work interchangeably, so it was important for us to make them easy to use.\\n\\nYou can turn the button component into an anchor by simply passing the `href` prop. When the `href` prop is present, the button component uses the `Link` component from React Aria under the hood instead of `Button`. This means you can pass any props or attributes that an anchor tag would accept when `href` is provided.\\n\\n### [Link color buttons example](about:blank\\\\#link-color-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Link color buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#link-color-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\n[Button sm](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button md](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button lg](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button xl](https://www.untitledui.com/)\\n\\n### [Link gray buttons](about:blank\\\\#link-gray-buttons)\\n\\n### [Link gray buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#link-gray-buttons)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\n[Button sm](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button md](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button lg](https://www.untitledui.com/) [Button xl](https://www.untitledui.com/)\\n\\n### [Icon leading buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-leading-buttons)\\n\\nOur button component accepts the `iconLeading` and `iconTrailing` props to add an icon to the button. These icons should be either a function component that accepts a `className` prop or a JSX element with a `data-icon` attribute.\\n\\nIn server components, where you cannot directly pass a function component as a prop, we recommend passing a JSX element with a `data-icon` attribute to apply the button icon styles to the icon.\\n\\n### [Icon leading buttons example](about:blank\\\\#icon-leading-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Icon leading buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-leading-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Icon trailing buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-trailing-buttons)\\n\\nSimilar to the `iconLeading` prop, you can display a trailing icon by passing a function component or a JSX element with a `data-icon` attribute to the `iconTrailing` prop.\\n\\n### [Icon trailing buttons example](about:blank\\\\#icon-trailing-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Icon trailing buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-trailing-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Icon only buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-only-buttons)\\n\\nYou can also display an icon only button by omitting the button children and passing an icon to the `iconLeading` or `iconTrailing` prop.\\n\\nPlease note that, it's recommended to pass an `aria-label` to the button when using an icon only button in order to provide a better experience for screen readers as the icons themselves do not provide any context.\\n\\n### [Icon only buttons example](about:blank\\\\#icon-only-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Icon only buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#icon-only-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\n### [Loading buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#loading-buttons)\\n\\nOur button component makes it easy to implement loading states with the `isLoading` prop. When enabled, the button automatically displays a spinner and becomes non-interactive to prevent multiple submissions. You can control whether to show the button text alongside the spinner using the `showTextWhileLoading` prop—this is particularly useful for providing context about what's happening. The loading spinner is automatically sized and styled to match your button's size and color variant.\\n\\n### [Loading buttons example](about:blank\\\\#loading-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Loading buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#loading-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Disabled buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#disabled-buttons)\\n\\nOur button component handles disabled states through the `isDisabled` prop, which applies appropriate styling and removes all interactive functionality. When disabled, buttons automatically change their appearance to indicate that they are not clickable, change their cursor to a not-allowed cursor, and any associated actions or navigation are prevented. The disabled styling is consistent across all button variants and sizes, ensuring a cohesive user experience throughout your application while maintaining accessibility standards.\\n\\n### [Disabled buttons example](about:blank\\\\#disabled-buttons-example)\\n\\n### [Disabled buttons example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#disabled-buttons-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xlButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Primary buttons destructive](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#primary-buttons-destructive)\\n\\nOur button component provides three destructive variants: `primary-destructive`, `secondary-destructive`, and `tertiary-destructive`. Each variant maintains the same interaction patterns as their non-destructive counterparts while applying appropriate warning colors and styling. You can use these variants just like regular buttons, and they support all the same features including icons, loading states, and disabled states, ensuring consistency across your application's destructive actions.\\n\\n### [Primary buttons destructive example](about:blank\\\\#primary-buttons-destructive-example)\\n\\n### [Primary buttons destructive example](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#primary-buttons-destructive-example)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Secondary buttons destructive](about:blank\\\\#secondary-buttons-destructive)\\n\\n### [Secondary buttons destructive](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#secondary-buttons-destructive)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n### [Tertiary buttons destructive](about:blank\\\\#tertiary-buttons-destructive)\\n\\n### [Tertiary buttons destructive](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons\\\\#tertiary-buttons-destructive)\\n\\nPreview\\n\\nCode\\n\\nButton smButton mdButton lgButton xl\\n\\n## FAQs\\n\\nPlease refer to our [frequently asked questions](https://www.untitledui.com/faqs) page for more.\\n\\n### What colors are available for buttons?\\n\\nButtons support multiple color variants including `\\\"primary\\\"`, `\\\"secondary\\\"`, `\\\"tertiary\\\"`, `\\\"primary-destructive\\\"`, `\\\"secondary-destructive\\\"`, `\\\"tertiary-destructive\\\"`, `\\\"link-gray\\\"`, `\\\"link-color\\\"`, and `\\\"link-destructive\\\"`. The default color is `\\\"primary\\\"`.\\n\\n### What sizes are available for buttons?\\n\\nButtons support four sizes: `\\\"sm\\\"` (small), `\\\"md\\\"` (medium), `\\\"lg\\\"` (large), and `\\\"xl\\\"` (extra large). You can set the size using the `size` prop, with `\\\"sm\\\"` being the default size.\\n\\n### Can I use custom icons with buttons?\\n\\nYes, you can add icons using the `iconLeading` or `iconTrailing` props. These props accept either React components or React elements. Icons are automatically sized and styled to match the button size.\\n\\n### How do I disable a button?\\n\\nYou can disable a button by setting the `isDisabled` prop to `true`. This will apply disabled styling, prevent user interaction, and disable any associated links or actions.\\n\\n### Can I use buttons for navigation?\\n\\nYes, you can use buttons for navigation by providing an `href` prop. When an `href` is provided, the button renders as an anchor tag while maintaining button styling.\\n\\n### How do I handle loading states for buttons?\\n\\nYou can handle loading states using the `isLoading` prop. When set to `true`, the button displays a loading spinner and becomes non-interactive. Use `showTextWhileLoading` to keep the button text visible during loading.\\n\\n### How do I make a button take the full width of its container?\\n\\nYou can make a button take the full width by adding the `w-full` Tailwind class to the `className` prop. The button component accepts standard HTML attributes and Tailwind classes.\\n\\n[Button groups](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/button-groups) [Checkboxes](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/checkboxes)\\n\\nOn this page\\n\\n- [Installation](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#installation)\\n- [Button examples](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#button-examples)\\n- [Primary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#primary-buttons)\\n- [Secondary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#secondary-buttons)\\n- [Tertiary buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#tertiary-buttons)\\n- [Link color buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#link-color-buttons)\\n- [Link gray buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#link-gray-buttons)\\n- [Icon leading buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-leading-buttons)\\n- [Icon trailing buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-trailing-buttons)\\n- [Icon only buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-only-buttons)\\n- [Loading buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#loading-buttons)\\n- [Disabled buttons](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#disabled-buttons)\\n- [Primary buttons destructive](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#primary-buttons-destructive)\\n- [Secondary buttons destructive](https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#secondary-buttons-destructive)\\n- [Tertiary buttons hover:*:data-icon:text-fg-quaternary_hover\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" type=\\\"button\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" id=\\\"react-aria-_R_3mq6qnpknpfiiv7b_\\\"><span data-text=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"transition-inherit-all px-0.5\\\">Button xl</span></button></div></div></div>\\n<h3 id=\\\"link-color-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#link-color-buttons\\\">Link color buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>We've designed our button components to be \\\"hybrid,\\\" meaning they can function as either a link or a standard button with ease. This offers several benefits, for example, there are many situations where you might want a link that behaves like a button, or a button that acts as a link. These two essential components work interchangeably, so it was important for us to make them easy to use.</p>\\n<p>You can turn the button component into an anchor by simply passing the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">href</code> prop. When the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">href</code> prop is present, the button component uses the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">Link</code> component from React Aria under the hood instead of <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">Button</code>. This means you can pass any props or attributes that an anchor tag would accept when <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">href</code> is provided.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a class=\\\"rounded-xs outline-focus-ring focus:outline-2 focus:outline-offset-2\\\" href=\\\"about:blank#link-color-buttons-example\\\">Link color buttons example</a></h3></div><div class=\\\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 md:h-9\\\"><div class=\\\"flex\\\"></div></div></header><div id=\\\"link-color-buttons-example\\\" data-preview=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"group flex w-full scroll-mt-20 flex-col gap-3 in-data-docs:my-8 not-typography\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" data-orientation=\\\"horizontal\\\" style=\\\"--preview-height: 18px;\\\"><header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a class=\\\"rounded-xs outline-focus-ring focus:outline-2 gap-1.5 *:data-text:underline-offset-4\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\"><span data-text=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"transition-inherit-all\\\">Button xl</span></a></div></div></div>\\n<h3 id=\\\"icon-leading-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-leading-buttons\\\">Icon leading buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>Our button component accepts the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconLeading</code> and <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconTrailing</code> props to add an icon to the button. These icons should be either a function component that accepts a <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">className</code> prop or a JSX element with a <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">data-icon</code> attribute.</p>\\n<p>In server components, where you cannot directly pass a function component as a prop, we recommend passing a JSX element with a <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">data-icon</code> attribute to apply the button icon styles to the icon.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a class=\\\"rounded-xs outline-focus-ring focus:outline-2 focus:outline-offset-2\\\" href=\\\"about:blank#icon-leading-buttons-example\\\">Icon leading buttons example</a></h3></div><div class=\\\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 md:h-9\\\"><div class=\\\"flex\\\"></div></div></header><div id=\\\"icon-leading-buttons-example\\\" data-preview=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"group flex w-full scroll-mt-20 flex-col gap-3 in-data-docs:my-8 id=\\\"icon-trailing-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-trailing-buttons\\\">Icon trailing buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>Similar to the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconLeading</code> prop, you can display a trailing icon by passing a function component or a JSX element with a <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">data-icon</code> attribute to the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconTrailing</code> prop.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 data-text=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"transition-inherit-all px-0.5\\\">Button lg</span><svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" hover:*:data-icon:text-fg-quaternary_hover\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" type=\\\"button\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" id=\\\"react-aria-_R_amqdanpknpfiiv7b_\\\"><span data-text=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"transition-inherit-all px-0.5\\\">Button xl</span><svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" data-icon=\\\"true\\\"><path d=\\\"M12 22c5.523 0 10-4.477 10-10S17.523 2 12 2 2 6.477 2 12s4.477 10 10 10Z\\\"></path></svg></button></div></div></div>\\n<h3 id=\\\"icon-only-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-only-buttons\\\">Icon only buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>You can also display an icon only button by omitting the button children and passing an icon to the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconLeading</code> or <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">iconTrailing</code> prop.</p>\\n<p>Please note that, it's recommended to pass an <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">aria-label</code> to the button when using an icon only button in order to provide a better experience for screen readers as the icons themselves do not provide any context.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a class=\\\"rounded-xs outline-focus-ring focus:outline-2 focus:outline-offset-2\\\" href=\\\"about:blank#icon-only-buttons-example\\\">Icon only buttons example</a></h3></div><div class=\\\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 md:h-9\\\"><div hover:*:data-icon:text-fg-quaternary_hover\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" type=\\\"button\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" aria-label=\\\"Button CTA\\\" id=\\\"react-aria-_R_66qfanpknpfiiv7b_\\\"><svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" 10Z\\\"></path></svg></button></div></div></div>\\n<h3 id=\\\"loading-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#loading-buttons\\\">Loading buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>Our button component makes it easy to implement loading states with the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">isLoading</code> prop. When enabled, the button automatically displays a spinner and becomes non-interactive to prevent multiple submissions. You can control whether to show the button text alongside the spinner using the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">showTextWhileLoading</code> prop—this is particularly useful for providing context about what's happening. The loading spinner is automatically sized and styled to match your button's size and color variant.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a class=\\\"rounded-xs outline-focus-ring focus:outline-2 focus:outline-offset-2\\\" href=\\\"about:blank#loading-buttons-example\\\">Loading buttons example</a></h3></div><div class=\\\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 md:h-9\\\"><div class=\\\"flex\\\"></div></div></header><div id=\\\"loading-buttons-example\\\" data-preview=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"group flex w-full scroll-mt-20 flex-col gap-3 in-data-docs:my-8 not-typography\\\" font-semibold before:rounded-[7px] data-icon-only:p-3.5 text-tertiary hover:bg-primary_hover hover:text-tertiary_hover data-loading:bg-primary_hover *:data-icon:text-fg-quaternary hover:*:data-icon:text-fg-quaternary_hover pointer-events-none [&amp;>*:not([data-icon=loading]):not([data-text])]:hidden\\\" data-rac=\\\"\\\" type=\\\"button\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" aria-disabled=\\\"true\\\" id=\\\"react-aria-_R_amqgqnpknpfiiv7b_\\\" data-pending=\\\"true\\\"><svg fill=\\\"none\\\" data-icon=\\\"loading\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 20 20\\\" class=\\\"pointer-events-none size-5 shrink-0 transition-inherit-all\\\"><circle class=\\\"stroke-current opacity-30\\\" cx=\\\"10\\\" cy=\\\"10\\\" r=\\\"8\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\"></circle><circle class=\\\"origin-center animate-spin stroke-current\\\" cx=\\\"10\\\" cy=\\\"10\\\" r=\\\"8\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-dasharray=\\\"12.5 50\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\"></circle></svg><span data-text=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"transition-inherit-all px-0.5\\\">Button xl</span></button></div></div></div>\\n<h3 id=\\\"disabled-buttons\\\" class=\\\"scroll-mt-20\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#disabled-buttons\\\">Disabled buttons</a></h3>\\n<p>Our button component handles disabled states through the <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">isDisabled</code> prop, which applies appropriate styling and removes all interactive functionality. When disabled, buttons automatically change their appearance to indicate that they are not clickable, change their cursor to a not-allowed cursor, and any associated actions or navigation are prevented. The disabled styling is consistent across all button variants and sizes, ensuring a cohesive user experience throughout your application while maintaining accessibility standards.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#primary-buttons-destructive\\\">Primary buttons destructive</a></h3>\\n<p>Our button component provides three destructive variants: <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">primary-destructive</code>, <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">secondary-destructive</code>, and <code class=\\\"nowrap cursor-pointer transition duration-100 ease-linear hover:text-brand-secondary max-md:break-all\\\" data-react-aria-pressable=\\\"true\\\" tabindex=\\\"0\\\">tertiary-destructive</code>. Each variant maintains the same interaction patterns as their non-destructive counterparts while applying appropriate warning colors and styling. You can use these variants just like regular buttons, and they support all the same features including icons, loading states, and disabled states, ensuring consistency across your application's destructive actions.</p>\\n<header class=\\\"flex w-full flex-col justify-between gap-3 md:flex-row md:items-center\\\"><div class=\\\"flex items-center gap-3\\\"><h3 class=\\\"text-md font-semibold last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-0\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">What colors are available for buttons?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-0\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">Buttons support multiple color variants including <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"primary\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"secondary\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"tertiary\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"primary-destructive\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"secondary-destructive\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"tertiary-destructive\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"link-gray\\\"</code>, <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"link-color\\\"</code>, and <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"link-destructive\\\"</code>. The default color is <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"primary\\\"</code>.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-1\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">What sizes are available for buttons?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-1\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">Buttons support four sizes: <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"sm\\\"</code> (small), <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"md\\\"</code> (medium), <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"lg\\\"</code> (large), and <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"xl\\\"</code> (extra large). You can set the size using the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">size</code> prop, with <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">\\\"sm\\\"</code> being the default size.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-2\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">Can I use custom icons with buttons?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-2\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">Yes, you can add icons using the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">iconLeading</code> or <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">iconTrailing</code> props. These props accept either React components or React elements. Icons are automatically sized and styled to match the button size.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-3\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">How do I disable a button?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-3\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">You can disable a button by setting the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">isDisabled</code> prop to <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">true</code>. This will apply disabled styling, prevent user interaction, and disable any associated links or actions.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-4\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">Can I use buttons for navigation?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-4\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">Yes, you can use buttons for navigation by providing an <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">href</code> prop. When an <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">href</code> is provided, the button renders as an anchor tag while maintaining button styling.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-5\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">How do I handle loading states for buttons?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-5\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">You can handle loading states using the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">isLoading</code> prop. When set to <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">true</code>, the button displays a loading spinner and becomes non-interactive. Use <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">showTextWhileLoading</code> to keep the button text visible during loading.</p></div></div></div><div class=\\\"-mt-px border-t border-secondary pt-6 last:border-b last:pb-6\\\"><h3><button aria-expanded=\\\"false\\\" aria-controls=\\\"faq-answer-6\\\" class=\\\"flex w-full cursor-pointer items-start justify-between gap-2 rounded-md text-left outline-focus-ring select-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 md:gap-6\\\"><span class=\\\"text-md font-semibold text-primary\\\">How do I make a button take the full width of its container?</span><span aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\" class=\\\"flex size-6 items-center text-fg-quaternary\\\"><svg width=\\\"24\\\" height=\\\"24\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"><line class=\\\"origin-center rotate-0 transition duration-150 ease-out\\\" x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"5\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"19\\\"></line><line x1=\\\"5\\\" y1=\\\"12\\\" x2=\\\"19\\\" y2=\\\"12\\\"></line></svg></span></button></h3><div id=\\\"faq-answer-6\\\" role=\\\"region\\\" class=\\\"overflow-hidden\\\" style=\\\"height:0px;opacity:0\\\"><div class=\\\"pt-2 pr-8 pb-0.5 md:pr-12\\\"><p class=\\\"my-4 first:mt-0 last:mb-0 text-md text-tertiary\\\">You can make a button take the full width by adding the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">w-full</code> Tailwind class to the <code class=\\\"text-sm font-mono nowrap text-tertiary font-bold bg-secondary ring-1 ring-inset ring-secondary rounded-md px-1.5 py-0.5\\\">className</code> prop. The button component accepts standard HTML attributes and Tailwind classes.</p></div></div></div></div></div><!--$--><!--/$--></div><div class=\\\"flex w-full items-center mt-24\\\"><a class=\\\"group relative inline-flex h-max cursor-pointer items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap outline-brand transition duration-100 ease-linear before:absolute focus-visible:outline-2 in-data-input-wrapper:in-data-leading:before:rounded-r-none in-data-input-wrapper:in-data-trailing:-ml-px in-data-input-wrapper:in-data-trailing:rounded-l-none class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#link-color-buttons\\\" data-id=\\\"link-color-buttons\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Link color buttons</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#link-gray-buttons\\\" data-id=\\\"link-gray-buttons\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Icon leading buttons</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#icon-trailing-buttons\\\" data-id=\\\"icon-trailing-buttons\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Icon trailing buttons</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#loading-buttons\\\" data-id=\\\"loading-buttons\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Loading buttons</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#disabled-buttons\\\" data-id=\\\"disabled-buttons\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Disabled buttons</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#primary-buttons-destructive\\\" data-id=\\\"primary-buttons-destructive\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Primary buttons destructive</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#secondary-buttons-destructive\\\" data-id=\\\"secondary-buttons-destructive\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-quaternary\\\">Secondary buttons destructive</a></li><li class=\\\"flex\\\"><a href=\\\"https://www.untitledui.com/react/components/buttons#tertiary-buttons-destructive\\\" data-id=\\\"tertiary-buttons-destructive\\\" class=\\\"text-sm font-semibold text-brand-secondary\\\">Tertiary buttons destructive</a></li></ul></div><div class=\\\"sticky inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-1 flex h-0 max-h-0 items-end\\\"><div class=\\\"pointer-events-none z-1 h-8 w-full bg-linear-to-t from-bg-primary to-transparent\\\"></div></div></div></div></div></div></div>\\n<next-route-announcer style=\\\"position: absolute;\\\"><div aria-live=\\\"assertive\\\" id=\\\"__next-route-announcer__\\\" role=\\\"alert\\\" style=\\\"position: absolute; border: 0px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0px; width: 1px; clip: rect(0px, 0px, 0px, 0px); overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap; overflow-wrap: normal;\\\"></div></next-route-announcer></body></html>\"\n}"
  }
]
